mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth and consumer spending acceleration that boosts corporate revenue expectations and lifts equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts and industrial production indicates broad-based demand expansion. When both series exceed their respective 10-year medians, equity cash flow growth accelerates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion with capacity constraints, rising industrial production signals supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins. This margin compression forces equity multiples to contract, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production prints above 103.0 while S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Real economic growth outpaces headline inflation when quarterly GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent while the CPI index remains below 2.5 percent annualized. This disinflationary growth regime expands corporate profit margins and lifts equity valuations over the following month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.5% and CPIAUCSL annualized inflation < 2.5%
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative median forward return across all triggered windows in the 2010-2020 sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals sustained economic momentum. This employment expansion reinforces corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of monthly PAYEMS changes against 60-day forward SPX returns drops below 0.02 for three consecutive calendar years.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monetary tightening combined with accelerating inflation erodes real disposable income and increases borrowing costs. When the federal funds rate rises by more than 25 basis points in a single meeting while CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent, equity risk premiums spike and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2% over the subsequent 5 trading days in over 50% of instances where both proxies breach the specified thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in residential investment and consumer credit availability signals a credit crunch. When RSAFS declines by more than 5 percent over a three-month window, downstream economic activity slows, depressing corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered episodes despite the credit contraction signal.
